name: Education
tagline: Plan lessons, build materials, and give feedback with agents that keep student data private.
heroTitle: More teaching, less prep, with student data kept on-device.
heroSubtitle: Plan lessons, generate practice sets, and draft feedback with agents that run on your machine and ground answers in your own curriculum. Student information stays on-device by default, and every model call is governed and logged.
intro: Educators spend evenings on prep, grading drafts, and paperwork while student records demand strict privacy. Ryu runs capable agents locally so student data stays on-device, grounds answers in your own curriculum, and drafts materials and feedback you review. It supports the teacher, it never grades or decides on its own.

highlights.title: Student data stays on-device
highlights.description: Run a local model with no telemetry by default, so student names and work never leave the machine unless you allow it.

highlights.title: Grounded in your curriculum
highlights.description: Spaces and RAG let agents build from your own standards, textbooks, and rubrics and cite the source.

highlights.title: Reusable skills
highlights.description: Save your best prompts as Skills, like a lesson-plan builder or rubric grader, and reuse them across classes.

highlights.title: Governed and logged
highlights.description: Every model call passes through the Gateway with PII redaction and an audit trail, so use stays accountable.

useCases.title: Lesson planning
useCases.description: Generate a lesson plan aligned to your standards with objectives, activities, and timing for you to adapt.

useCases.title: Practice and quiz generation
useCases.description: Create differentiated practice sets and quiz questions at multiple levels from a single topic or reading.

useCases.title: Feedback drafts
useCases.description: Draft constructive, rubric-aligned feedback on a student submission that the teacher reviews and personalizes.

useCases.title: Material adaptation
useCases.description: Rewrite a passage at a different reading level or translate instructions to support multilingual learners.

useCases.title: Curriculum Q&A
useCases.description: Answer questions across your textbooks and standards with citations, so staff find the right reference fast.

useCases.title: Parent communication
useCases.description: Draft clear, warm progress notes and newsletter blurbs in the right tone for staff to send.

examplePrompts: Build a 45-minute lesson plan on photosynthesis aligned to our grade-7 standards, with objectives and a quick check.
Create a 10-question quiz on this reading with three difficulty levels.
Draft rubric-aligned feedback on this essay, highlighting two strengths and two next steps.
Rewrite this passage at a lower reading level for English-language learners.
Draft a short, encouraging progress note for a student improving in math participation.

faq.a: Ryu is local-first with no telemetry, so student names and work can stay entirely on-device. When you route to a cloud model the Gateway redacts sensitive data and logs the call. This helps you keep data on-device and governed; it is not a regulatory certification.

faq.q: Does Ryu grade students for me?
faq.a: No. Ryu drafts rubric-aligned feedback and practice materials. The teacher reviews, adjusts, and assigns every grade. Ryu speeds the prep, the educator stays in charge.

faq.q: Can it use our own curriculum and standards?
faq.a: Yes. Ground agents on your textbooks, standards, and rubrics with Spaces and RAG so materials and answers align to what you teach and cite the source.

ctaTitle: Give every educator an evening back.
ctaSubtitle: Plan, generate, and give feedback faster while student data stays on-device and governed.
ctaNote: On-device by default · Curriculum-grounded · Full audit trail
